title: 11 of 13 tests skip when data/kaggle is absent; line coverage only 5.1%
evidence: soccer/soccer_test.go:17 t.Skipf("data dir not available") gates loadDB; data/kaggle is not in the archive, so only TestNormalizeTeam and TestParseDate run (test_coverage=0.051, defect_rate=1.0 from scores.json). soccer_test.go:182 t.Skip("no matches") is a second data-gated skip.
suggestion: Ship a small fixture CSV (a few rows per competition) so the query/loader tests execute deterministically without the full Kaggle datasets.

title: Match filtering exposes season/year but not an explicit date range
evidence: main.go:55 matches_by_team accepts season+competition only; soccer/query.go:83 MatchesBySeason exists but no date-range query/tool is wired up. R4 ('date range and/or season') is satisfied via season.
suggestion: Optionally add from/to date arguments to matches_by_team for full date-range coverage.

title: MCP protocol implemented by hand rather than via an official SDK
evidence: main.go:15-263 defines rpcRequest/rpcResponse and a stdio JSON-RPC loop directly (initialize, tools/list, tools/call, ping). Functionally satisfies R1 but is not built on the MCP SDK.
suggestion: Acceptable for tooling=none/stdlib-only; note for cross-run comparison against SDK-based implementations.
